fact: November 13 is exactly 47 days before New Year's Eve - that's almost 7 weeks of waiting for the countdown!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying their winter stash - they can bury up to 25 nuts per hour!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities by November 13, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
This date falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign known for being mysterious and having a stinger!
In Australia and other southern places, November 13 is when jacaranda trees explode with purple flowers that carpet the ground like nature's confetti.
Why do cats get extra fluffy around this time? Their winter coats are growing in thick and fast, making them look like furry marshmallows.
November 13 sits exactly 317 days into the year - leaving just 48 days until the calendar flips to January 1st.
In northern forests, this is peak time for trees to drop their leaves in massive piles - some oak trees can dump 200,000 leaves!
The moon phase around November 13 varies wildly each year, but when it's a full moon, it rises earlier and stays up longer than summer full moons.
Bears in cold regions are getting seriously sleepy by this date - they're entering hyperphagia, eating up to 20,000 calories per day!
Morning frost becomes a regular visitor in temperate zones around November 13, turning spider webs into glittery masterpieces.
This date marks roughly 6 weeks until the winter solstice - we're sliding fast toward the shortest day of the year in the north!
